title: Eva 04s pilot in the series
wikitext:
[]: Eva-04's pilot in the series             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 24, 2009 4:53 pm This is probably going to be more of a "what if" type of thread then anything substantial from the series, but a thought just struck me. (I blame the Eva2 game and the RP thread for making me actually think! ) What's the possibility that Eva-04's pilot in Nevada was none other then Kaworu? I'm not saying he necessarily had anything to do with causing the disappearance of Nevada's branch into the Sea of Dirac (although it's a possibility, despite not knowing the reasons why), but couldn't it be possible that Eva-04's pilot (since the Evas need pilots to function) was a character we actually know about? Granted, the whole situation with Eva-04 didn't seem to be extremely important to the original plot in the grand scheme of things (with the exception of learning a bit more about the Sea of Dirac as well as Eva-03 being transported to Nerv Headquarters as a result), I just thought this idea was interesting. (And if for whatever reason it seems that important, it's possible Rebuild 2.0 will shed more light on this subject. Like I mentioned before, maybe glasses girl Mari would have to do with it. Although even that's hard to tell at this point.)

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Feb 24, 2009 4:57 pm This idea is bad and you should feel bad. We are all dumber for having read this. ^__^

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Feb 24, 2009 4:59 pm I think Kensuke would have pilot Unit 04 since Kensuke said that he does not have a family and maybe his Mother soul is inside of the Unit.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 24, 2009 5:00 pm One word, your honor: "dummy plug". I say this not just for the sake of making a dumb joke, but because the subtitles for the incident talk about synchronization data, when another "Children" obviously had not been appointed by the Marduk Institute yet. Seele was presumably working on their own dummy system at this time, so instead of bothering with any pilots for the purpose of the experiment, they just sent 2nd Branch a copy, and nothing critical was lost. Well, except an Eva, an S2 Engine, and an entire branch of Nerv. Not sure why synchronization played any role in the experiment at all, to be perfectly honest.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Tue Feb 24, 2009 5:02 pm Well, it's white like Kaworu. And all Evas share color a color scheme with their pilot with the notable exception of Eva01 and the original Eva00. So it's definitely, possibly a maybe.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 24, 2009 5:13 pm Reichu: Ah, the dummy plug eh? That works. It's just I was under the impression there needed to be a pilot in the Eva for the dummy plug to function properly? Somebody correct me if I'm wrong of course. One more thing I wanted to mention: I know crappy theory is crappy, but keep in mind Kaworu being the pilot if it was needed wouldn't have been too much of a stretch. For one thing, he could have left the plug before the Eva and branch disappeared (being an Angel and all I'm sure he'd have some trick up his sleeve). Also, Kaworu being flown over from Germany(?) to the states wouldn't be that unlikely either.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Feb 24, 2009 5:28 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I was under the impression there needed to be a pilot in the Eva for the dummy plug to function properly

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Tue Feb 24, 2009 6:30 pm So,ebody correct me if I'm wrong, but wasnt Kensuke the Pilot of Unit 04 in the PS2 game, depending on what actions you take? Although Kensuke as an Eva pilot would either go really well... since he's an army fanboy... or really badly, since he's an army fanboy... He may be a Leeroy, he may be competant.... As an aside, I've been messing with the idea in a fanfic of mine that Kawaoru for some reason is the Pilot of Unit 04... because it would certainly make for an interesting storyline.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 24, 2009 6:51 pm I believe Kensuke was the pilot of Eva-04 in Battle Orchestra but I'm not completely certain.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Feb 24, 2009 7:30 pm In terms of the TV series the pilot of Unit-04 could be either Kensuke, Hikari, or Kaworu in my opinion. Although for Kaworu being a pilot of an EVA it would be more or less. If Kaworu wasn't an angel and/ or if he had received his own EVA Unit before he died he would probably pilot one of the Mass Produced Eva like in Girlfriend of Steel 2. It would've been Kensuke most likely though, seeing as how Toji was chosen for Unit-03. As for Hikari... She might've been chosen because she was shown in the series from time to time, even though she didn't really effect the plot.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 24, 2009 7:43 pm Okey dokey, now that I'm back home I can post a script excerpt: Episode 17 (Platinum) wrote: IBUKI (OFF): Just the schedule for Unit 04 is fine. I'd like confirmation on the synchro data and the S2 engine.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 24, 2009 8:48 pm Reichu wrote: Why on Earth would Seele send their precious 17th Angel out from under their thumb, before the 'appointed time', for something as menial and potentially dangerous -- in more than one way -- as this?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 24, 2009 8:54 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I was starting to think that maybe Kaworu caused the Dirac Sea.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 24, 2009 9:04 pm Most likely that I completely forgot the disappearance was a case of "messing with things humans don't understand". Still, I'm glad there was clarification in this topic that Eva-04 didn't NEED a pilot since the fandom enjoys creating one.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Feb 24, 2009 11:59 pm sorry to go off topic but one quick question Was it confirmed that eva 03 had an s2 engine? If so why was it destroyed so much more easily than the mass produced models? or does yui just kick that much ass?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Wed Feb 25, 2009 12:24 am I've obviously thought about this question a lot. So here is my thoughts on the subject: there is not enough information to answer the question. Some material such as the games would have you believe that the next pilot would be Kensuki or Hikari, but all that is non-canon and has no bearing on the plot. The most simple explanation would be that it only has a dummy plug, as Reichu has suggested, but again, there is no evidence in favor for that-- in fact, I personally rejected that theory when I considered the fact that the dummy plug system was a new development that hadn't been tested yet until the following episode, so they logically shouldn't yet have a dummy plug to put in Unit 04. At least, not a working one. It just doesn't fit chronologically, even considering the ludicrous speed at which new technologies are developed in the Eva universe. Besides, if they were going to use a dummy plug in Unit 04, why wouldn't they do the same with Unit 03? After all, they were both finished in the same timeframe, from what we do know. It isn't consistent. My theory, despite my fanfiction to the contrary, is that they actually didn't yet have a pilot for Unit 04. They had Unit 03 all ready to go, but it didn't have a pilot until it got to Nerv central. Why should Unit 04 be any different? Although it doesn't answer the question of what they were doing the day the second branch was lost, I think it the most plausible theory considering the circumstances and precedent set by Unit 03's activation. Note, this obviously isn't an obstacle for fic writers, or even Gianax itself. Its just vauge enough that any interpretation is a correct interpretation in that sphere.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 25, 2009 1:20 am Axell wrote: Was it confirmed that eva 03 had an s2 engine?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Wed Feb 25, 2009 1:59 am Reichu wrote: Formless One wrote: The most simple explanation would be that it only has a dummy plug, as Reichu has suggested, but again, there is no evidence in favor for that [since] the dummy plug system was a new development that hadn't been tested yet until the following episode, so they logically shouldn't yet have a dummy plug to put in Unit 04.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Wed Feb 25, 2009 2:22 am I'm one of those who have written FanFics on EVA-04, so I can at least attest to doing some research on this topic. The series gives painfully little info on what, if any, pilot EVA-04 actually had. Personally, I'd go for the Kaworu Dummy Plug theory, except that Gendo was aware of the circumstances of the test (per his conversation with Futsuyuki after the disaster), and very easily prescribed it as an accident and not some sort of Angel-related event. If they were using a Dummy Plug other than Rei, Gendo would probably have known it and said something. The most logical reason IMHO is that there was no pilot at all. The scientists at 2nd Branch were hot-wiring the EVA with the Angel's S2 and messed up somewhere. Nevada is no stranger to messing around with alien technology, and the fact that 2nd Branch is located at Groom Lake is not a little ironic. Of course, there's always my theory...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 25, 2009 2:28 am Reichu wrote: Axell wrote: Was it confirmed that eva 03 had an s2 engine?
